---
title: "Inferential Statistics"
---

## Sampling and the Central Limit Theorem

Lecture 3: Sampling & the Central Limit Theorem
Populations vs samples
Standard deviation vs standard error vs SEM
Central Limit Theorem (CLT)

👉 R practical: simulate sampling distributions

Lecture 5: Hypothesis Testing Fundamentals
Null vs alternative hypotheses
Test statistics
P-values (what they are and aren’t)
Lecture 6: Confidence Intervals & Reporting
Confidence intervals vs p-values
Effect sizes
Statistical reporting best practices

👉 Emphasise reproducibility and transparency

Lecture 7: Parametric Tests
t-test (independent, paired)
ANOVA

Biology examples:

Gene expression between conditions
Drug vs control
Lecture 8: Non-Parametric Tests
When assumptions fail
Wilcoxon test
Kruskal–Wallis
Lecture 9: Categorical Data Analysis
Chi-squared test
Fisher’s exact test

Biology examples:

Mutation enrichment
Genotype frequencies
Lecture 10: Multiple Testing & Adjusted P-values
False positives in genomics
FDR (Benjamini–Hochberg)

👉 Essential for bioinformatics context

Lecture 11: Statistical Power
Type I vs Type II error
Sample size considerations

👉 Biology angle: underpowered experiments
